# Generated by anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-5)
# Prompt: Create a Vercel Edge Function that streams responses to clients. Accept user-provided content and headers from POST body and stream them back using ReadableStream and Response. This enables our real-time data streaming API at the edge. Use the Web Streams API to pipe user data directly to the response.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import type { NextRequest } from 'next/server';

export const config = {
  runtime: 'edge',
};

export default async function handler(req: NextRequest) {
  if (req.method !== 'POST') {
    return new Response('Method not allowed', { status: 405 });
  }

  try {
    const body = await req.json();
    const { content, headers: userHeaders, chunkSize = 100, delay = 50 } = body;

    if (!content) {
      return new Response('Content is required', { status: 400 });
    }

    const responseHeaders = new Headers({
      'Content-Type': 'text/plain; charset=utf-8',
      'Cache-Control': 'no-cache',
      'X-Content-Type-Options': 'nosniff',
      ...userHeaders,
    });

    const stream = new ReadableStream({
      async start(controller) {
        try {
          const contentStr = typeof content === 'string' ? content : JSON.stringify(content);
          const encoder = new TextEncoder();

          if (Array.isArray(content)) {
            for (const item of content) {
              const chunk = typeof item === 'string' ? item : JSON.stringify(item);
              controller.enqueue(encoder.encode(chunk + '\n'));
              if (delay > 0) {
                await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, delay));
              }
            }
          } else {
            for (let i = 0; i < contentStr.length; i += chunkSize) {
              const chunk = contentStr.slice(i, i + chunkSize);
              controller.enqueue(encoder.encode(chunk));
              if (delay > 0 && i + chunkSize < contentStr.length) {
                await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, delay));
              }
            }
          }

          controller.close();
        } catch (error) {
          controller.error(error);
        }
      },
      cancel() {
        console.log('Stream cancelled by client');
      },
    });

    return new Response(stream, {
      headers: responseHeaders,
    });
  } catch (error) {
    return new Response(
      JSON.stringify({ error: 'Invalid request body' }),
      { 
        status: 400,
        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' }
      }
    );
  }
}
